evidence: Global mutable registries are embedded in shared foundational modules: `errorNotifier` is module state in `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` (lines 25-33), and Supabase client/instrumentation state is module state in `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` (lines 9-10, 19-27).

Runtime globals are written from the Supabase integration (`window.__supabase_client__`, `window.supabase`) in `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` (lines 53-61), which creates implicit cross-module coupling through ambient state.
A proxy-based compatibility layer (`export const supabase = new Proxy(...)`) in `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` (lines 63-87) hides initialization and dependency boundaries from call sites.
`src/shared/components` contains strongly domain-specific UI (`ActiveLoRAsDisplay`, `AspectRatioSelector`) alongside true cross-cutting components, making shared ownership boundaries ambiguous.
`src/shared/hooks/useApiTokens.ts` is account/API-token domain logic (auth session checks, `user_api_tokens` table, `generate-pat`/`revoke-pat` RPCs) but is placed in the global shared hook layer.

Test files still reference pre-reorg module locations (`@/shared/lib/errorHandler`, `@/shared/lib/queryKeys`) while production imports use newer segmented paths (`@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError`, `@/shared/lib/queryKeys/api`), indicating structural drift.

Vitest config entry points are split between root wrappers (`vitest.edge.config.ts`, `vitest.edge.smoke.config.ts`) and underlying Supabase function configs, increasing indirection for test ownership discovery.

Invalidation seam exposes two conflicting contracts: `useInvalidateGenerations` debounces and cancels per-shot timeouts, while `invalidateGenerationsSync` still schedules delayed async `setTimeout` work and cannot be cancelled (`src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts`).

Clip metadata integration is split across two paths with different behavior and observability: `clipInitService` reads duration through ad-hoc `<video>` probing and silently resolves `0`, while `clipManager/metadata` uses `extractVideoMetadataFromUrl` and centralized `handleError` (`src/tools/join-clips/lib/clipInitService.ts`, `src/tools/join-clips/lib/clipManager/metadata.ts`).

Two large form APIs flatten grouped props via spread composition (`resolveShotImagesEditorProps`, `resolveJoinClipsSettingsFormProps`), creating fragile cross-module handoffs where later groups can silently override earlier fields (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/types.ts`, `src/shared/components/JoinClipsSettingsForm/types.ts`).

Lineage boundary logic claims recursive traversal via `params.source_variant_id` but only fetches variants within the current `generation_id`, truncating cross-generation chains while failing closed to empty data (`src/shared/hooks/useLineageChain.ts`) despite generation flows carrying parent linkage (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts`).

Inline edit state construction has high local ceremony: `buildImageEditValueParams` in `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts` (lines 249-334) manually maps a very large field set that mirrors `UseImageEditValueParams` in `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts` (lines 18-127).

The same module then re-maps many of those values again into `InlineEditStateResult` return slices (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts` lines 442-509), increasing sync burden and accidental omission risk.

`useImageEditValue` memoization is ineffective as written: `useMemo(..., [params])` (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts` lines 240-244) receives a freshly created params object each render from `buildImageEditValueParams(...)` call site (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts` lines 438-440).

src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts keeps a dated sunset contract (remove by 2026-05-31) while still exposing LegacyProjectImageSettings with multiple @deprecated fields and widening active input via ProjectImageSettingsInput = ProjectImageSettings & Partial<LegacyProjectImageSettings> (lines 101-128).

src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts still imports legacy input types and widens ingestion type with Partial<LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput> (lines 3-7, 352-354), plus UI config keeps legacy guidance metadata fields for decoding (lines 123-131).

src/shared/lib/taskIdHelpers.ts and src/shared/lib/taskTypeUtils.ts preserve legacy aliases/fallback heuristics (`task_id` fallback and `taskType.includes('travel')`) instead of converging to canonical identifiers (taskIdHelpers lines 27-32; taskTypeUtils lines 46-48).

Task row translation logic is duplicated with near-identical field mapping in `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts` (`mapDbTaskToTask`) and `src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts` (`mapDbTaskRowToTask`), creating parallel abstractions for the same boundary.

`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts` builds and re-exports a very large composite state object while `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts` mirrors that shape again via a massive `UseImageEditValueParams` interface, increasing synchronization burden rather than reducing complexity.

Several helper layers in `useInlineEditState` are mostly pass-through adapters (environment/persistence/inpainting/magic/reposition/img2img/value builders), which increases abstraction count without proportionate domain compression.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` introduces a Proxy-based lazy singleton (`supabase`) with method rebinding and reflective traps, making runtime behavior less transparent than direct client access.

`src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts` exposes both a class singleton and a method-forwarding API object (`dataFreshnessManager`) that proxies every call to lazy instance creation, adding an extra forwarding layer at each call site.
Combined with broad usage (`supabase` and `dataFreshnessManager` imported widely), these indirections make debugging initialization/order issues more difficult.

`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts` types `taskData` as `unknown` but immediately reads `taskData.params`, `taskData.category`, and `taskData.task_type`, so the declared interface does not match actual assumptions.

`src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts` uses broad string payload fields for constrained semantics (e.g., `mobilePaneOpen.side: string | null`, realtime `priority: string`), which weakens event contract accuracy and allows invalid states through type checks.

`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts` and `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts` expose oversized interfaces that leak internal hook details rather than a narrower stable contract.

Client-side modules mix raw throws, `handleError`, and ad-hoc logging: e.g., `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` throws `new Error(...)`, logs with `console.error`, and also routes errors through `handleError` in mutation callbacks.

Task creation modules (`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`) follow `handleError(...); throw error` patterns, while other modules log-and-return silently.

Server completion paths (`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts`) frequently `console.error` and return `null`/early `return` for failures, creating different failure semantics from client modules that throw.

findings.summary: Shared core modules rely on mutable module-level registries and ambient globals, creating hidden cross-module coupling.
evidence: `handleError.ts` stores notifier state in module scope (`let errorNotifier`) and mutates it via `configureErrorNotifier` (lines 25-33), so behavior depends on external initialization order.
`client.ts` keeps singleton/instrumentation state in module scope (`instrumentationInstalled`, `clientInstance`) and mutates browser globals in `initializeSupabaseDebugGlobals` (lines 9-10, 53-61).
`client.ts` exports a proxy (`supabase`) that lazily initializes and binds methods at property access time (lines 63-87), obscuring runtime dependency direction.
findings.suggestion: Move notifier/client wiring to explicit app bootstrap composition: inject notifier/client dependencies into consumers (or a dedicated provider) and keep module exports side-effect free with explicit `init()` at entrypoint boundaries.

findings.summary: Mobile/touch interaction policy is duplicated across shared hooks, UI primitives, and feature hooks instead of one owned boundary.
evidence: `useMobile.tsx` defines UA + pointer + breakpoint detection and orientation logic (lines 9-56, 72-214).
`tooltip.tsx` adds independent touch toggling and outside-pointer closure logic in `TouchableTooltip` (lines 69-93), bypassing shared mobile policy.
`useMobileInteractions.ts` implements separate timing-based tap behavior and outside-click closure (lines 34-97), creating another independent mobile behavior model.
findings.suggestion: Create a single interaction policy layer (e.g., `useInteractionMode` + shared gesture helpers) and refactor tooltip/gallery logic to consume that layer; keep shared UI primitives focused on rendering/ARIA behavior.

findings.summary: Media gallery behavior is decomposed into broad prop/state bags, making ownership and change boundaries hard to reason about.
evidence: `useMediaGalleryHandlers` receives many unrelated concerns (navigation, filters, modal state, selection, lightbox state) in one interface (lines 16-29).
`ShotActions` expects three grouped state objects plus callbacks (`selector`, `status`, `quickCreate`, `actions`) and performs orchestration logic (lines 14-141).
`useMediaGalleryItemState` exposes another wide mutable state surface for the same feature area (lines 10-32, 75-97).
findings.suggestion: Introduce a `MediaGalleryController` boundary (context/store or reducer) that owns shot-selection/lightbox/action workflows and expose narrow intent-based methods to components.

findings.summary: Task DB-to-domain mapping is duplicated across hooks and bridge utilities.
evidence: `TaskDbRow` and camelCase mapping are implemented twice (`useTasks.ts` lines 47-61 + 277-293, `generationTaskBridge.ts` lines 9-23 + 25-43).
Both implementations map the same fields (`task_type`, `dependant_on`, `generation_started_at`, `generation_processed_at`) with near-identical logic.
findings.suggestion: Extract a single shared mapper/type (for example `src/shared/lib/tasks/taskRowMapper.ts`) and consume it from both modules; keep one contract for DB row normalization to remove divergence risk.

findings.summary: Travel payload shaping is split across separate builders with overlapping rules.
evidence: `buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` and `buildOrchestratorDetails`/`buildTaskParamsPayload` independently compose overlapping fields such as `seed_base`/`seed_to_use`, `motion_mode`, `amount_of_motion`, and `structure_guidance`.
Both paths include legacy cleanup behavior (`stripLegacyTravelStructureFields` in one path; manual deletion loops and `use_svi=false` in the other), which can drift under future schema changes.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared travel payload normalizer for common orchestrator fields (seed, motion, structure guidance, legacy stripping), then layer only mode-specific additions in each caller.

findings.summary: Inline edit state assembly uses oversized param bags and repeated manual field wiring.
evidence: `UseImageEditValueParams` defines a very large surface (lines 18-127), and caller-side `buildImageEditValueParams` mirrors it with a long manual mapping (lines 249-334).
The same hook returns another large hand-assembled object (`InlineEditStateResult` mapping, lines 442-509), repeating many references already present in `imageEditValue`.
findings.suggestion: Refactor to pass grouped slices (`mode`, `canvas`, `reposition`, `generation`) from sub-hooks directly and derive `InlineEditStateResult` from those slices, reducing duplicate manual wiring points.

findings.summary: Memoization in `useImageEditValue` is defeated by unstable object identity.
evidence: `useImageEditValue` memo depends on `[params]` (`useImageEditValue.ts` lines 240-244).
Caller creates a new params object each render via `buildImageEditValueParams(...)` (`useInlineEditState.ts` lines 438-440), so memo recomputes every render.
findings.suggestion: Either remove `useMemo` (if recompute cost is trivial) or make dependencies explicit and stable by constructing state from primitive deps inside `useImageEditValue` rather than depending on whole-object identity.

findings.summary: Generation invalidation APIs expose inconsistent lifecycle semantics, causing race-prone cache refresh behavior.
evidence: `useInvalidateGenerations` tracks and cancels per-shot delayed invalidations with `timeoutRefs`, but `invalidateGenerationsSync` still uses uncancellable `setTimeout` while its name implies immediate/synchronous behavior.
`timelineWriteQueue` explicitly serializes writes and surfaces active state/timeouts, yet invalidation timing is decoupled and may fire after write ordering assumptions have changed.
findings.suggestion: Unify on one invalidation contract: either always immediate and awaitable, or explicitly async/debounced with returned cancellation handles. Rename `invalidateGenerationsSync` to reflect async scheduling, and optionally integrate invalidation triggering with timeline write queue completion hooks.

findings.summary: Join-clips clip duration loading uses two divergent adapters, producing inconsistent errors and fallback values.
evidence: `clipInitService.getVideoDurationFromUrl` creates a DOM `<video>` and resolves `0` on `onerror` without structured logging context.
`clipManager/metadata.loadClipDuration` calls shared `extractVideoMetadataFromUrl` and routes failures through `handleError` with `clipId` context before returning `0`.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate duration extraction behind one shared service function returning a typed result (`ok/value/error`), then reuse it in both initialization and metadata refresh paths so fallback policy and logging are identical.

findings.summary: Prop-group flatteners rely on spread order, making integration seams brittle to silent key collisions.
evidence: `resolveShotImagesEditorProps` merges four objects via spread with no collision guards, so overlapping keys are silently overwritten by later groups.
`resolveJoinClipsSettingsFormProps` repeats the same pattern across `clipSettings`, `motionConfig`, and `uiState`, expanding the chance of accidental contract break when groups evolve.
findings.suggestion: Keep grouped props at call boundaries (avoid flattening), or add explicit namespacing/merge guards (compile-time utility types plus runtime assertion in development) to prevent silent override behavior.

findings.summary: Variant lineage traversal is scoped to one generation, conflicting with source-pointer semantics and silently truncating chains.
evidence: `useLineageChain.fetchLineageChain` fetches `currentVariant`, then loads only rows where `generation_id = currentVariant.generation_id`, but traversal follows `params.source_variant_id` which can reference variants outside that set.

`generateVideoService` sends `parent_generation_id` in request body, indicating cross-generation ancestry is part of generation workflow, making single-generation lineage resolution an unreliable seam.
findings.suggestion: Resolve lineage iteratively by fetching parent variant records by ID until null/visited, not by preloading a single generation. Return a typed partial-chain/error state so callers can distinguish 'no lineage' from 'incomplete lineage'.

findings.summary: Legacy settings fields remain part of the main accepted type surface past declared sunset, keeping dual payload paths active.
evidence: ImageGenerationForm types keeps `LegacyProjectImageSettings` with multiple `@deprecated` fields and still unions it into active ingestion via `ProjectImageSettingsInput` (types.ts lines 109-128), despite a documented remove-by date of 2026-05-31 (lines 104-107).

Travel types still accept `Partial<LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput>` in `TravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput` (types.ts lines 352-354) and preserve legacy guidance metadata fields in UI config (lines 124-131).
findings.suggestion: Introduce strict canonical input types at ingestion boundaries now, gate legacy decode behind one migration adapter, and remove legacy fields from exported public interfaces once telemetry confirms no legacy payloads.

findings.summary: Identifier helpers still normalize multiple legacy names and fuzzy matching, which prolongs migration debt and obscures canonical contracts.
evidence: `getSourceTaskId` still falls back through `source_task_id || orchestrator_task_id || task_id` (taskIdHelpers.ts lines 27-32), preserving old field names in active flow.
`isTravelTaskType` adds broad `taskType.includes('travel')` fallback (taskTypeUtils.ts lines 46-48), keeping non-canonical matching behavior alive.
taskConfig documents and uses compatibility fallbacks (`versioned compatibility registry`) when cache misses occur (taskConfig.ts lines 57-60, 105-108).
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical identifier schema per task path, add explicit migration read path with deprecation logging, and remove fallback branches after a bounded cutoff release.

findings.summary: Two separate helpers implement overlapping aspect-ratio fallback behavior, increasing drift risk for simple UI logic.
evidence: `getAspectRatioStyle` in image-utils parses project ratio and defaults to square (lines 58-66).
`getImageAspectRatioStyle` repeats the same project-ratio parse + square fallback logic after metadata checks (lines 61-69).
findings.suggestion: Extract a single shared `resolveAspectRatio` primitive and compose metadata lookup on top of it so fallback behavior is defined once.

findings.summary: Local implementations lean on repeated type assertions instead of explicit narrowing, reducing readability and trust in internal flow.
evidence: preloader casts `img` to `{ id: string }` for both loaded checks and status writes (lines 32, 57) rather than narrowing once with a typed guard.
delete-confirm hook exposes `deletingId` via `(deleteGenerationMutation.variables as string)` (line 64), relying on assertion over typed mutation variables.
findings.suggestion: Add small local type guards (`hasId`) and strongly type mutation variable generics so code paths use inferred types without assertions.

findings.summary: Domain-owned features are placed in `src/shared`, weakening clear package ownership boundaries.
evidence: `useApiTokens` embeds product/account behavior (Supabase auth checks, `user_api_tokens`, PAT function calls) rather than reusable hook primitives.
`ActiveLoRAsDisplay` and `AspectRatioSelector` are feature-specific composition components but live in top-level shared components.
These files depend on domain models/utilities (`LoraDisplayModel`, aspect-ratio catalog) instead of generic app-wide contracts.
findings.suggestion: Create feature-scoped packages (for example `src/features/account/hooks/useApiTokens.ts`, `src/features/lora/components/ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx`, `src/features/generation/components/AspectRatioSelector.tsx`) and keep `src/shared` for pure primitives with stable cross-feature reuse.

findings.summary: Hook tests mock legacy library paths that no longer match current package structure.
evidence: `useApiTokens.test.ts` mocks `@/shared/lib/errorHandler` and `@/shared/lib/queryKeys` while the hook imports `@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError` and `@/shared/lib/queryKeys/api`.
`useAutoSaveSettings.test.ts` repeats the same legacy mocks (`errorHandler`, flat `queryKeys` path).
This mismatch indicates tests are coupled to prior package layout and can silently stop validating intended boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Align test mocks with current import contracts (`@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError`, segmented `queryKeys/*` modules), and add a lightweight convention check preventing new tests from using deprecated shared-lib entry paths.

findings.summary: Edge-test configuration ownership is split across root proxy files and Supabase-local config files.
evidence: `vitest.edge.config.ts` and `vitest.edge.smoke.config.ts` are pass-through exports to `supabase/functions/*` configs.
Root also contains independent web test config (`vitest.config.ts`), so test config discovery requires following indirection and naming conventions.
No single explicit config index defines which suite owns which runtime layer.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate test config entrypoints under a single `config/testing/` (or similar) namespace with explicit naming (`vitest.web`, `vitest.edge.unit`, `vitest.edge.smoke`) and keep root files only as documented compatibility shims if CLI tooling requires them.

findings.summary: Supabase integration exposes multiple architectural entry paths (getter, proxy export, window globals), weakening boundary integrity and compatibility governance.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exports both `getSupabaseClient()` and `supabase` Proxy for backward compatibility.
The same file also reuses and writes `window.__supabase_client__` and `window.supabase` in debug/global flows, creating additional implicit access paths.
`README.md` does not define a compatibility/deprecation policy for these parallel contracts.
findings.suggestion: Define one primary Supabase access contract (prefer explicit getter/factory), mark proxy/global paths as deprecated with removal criteria, and document the policy in README architecture governance docs.

findings.summary: Global mutable error notifier introduces cross-module initialization coupling through hidden shared state.
evidence: `handleError.ts` stores notifier in module-scoped `let errorNotifier` and mutates it via `configureErrorNotifier`.
Error behavior (`shouldShowToast && errorNotifier`) depends on external registration order rather than explicit dependency injection at call sites.
A similar module-singleton pattern appears in `supabase/client.ts` (`clientInstance`, `instrumentationInstalled`), but README-level architecture contracts do not define singleton governance rules.
findings.suggestion: Move notifier wiring to explicit app bootstrap composition (pass notifier dependency into handler factory) and document a single pattern for singleton/global registration contracts.

findings.summary: Top-level documentation advertises architecture references but omits runtime contract governance for high-impact shared hubs.
evidence: `README.md` links structure and quality docs but provides no contract rules for shared barrel modules or compatibility surfaces.
`generationAndShots.ts` is labeled canonical and has very high import reach (blind packet: 206 importers), but no documented ownership/versioning constraints are tied to that role.
`utils.ts` is a generic shared barrel with high import reach (blind packet: 134 importers), yet no documented boundary on what belongs in this hub.
findings.suggestion: Add a governance section in README defining allowed shared hubs, barrel scope rules, compatibility/deprecation policy, and quality-gate checks for high-import surfaces.

findings.summary: Task DB-to-domain mapping is duplicated across modules, creating parallel abstractions for the same boundary.
evidence: `mapDbTaskToTask` in `useTasks.ts` and `mapDbTaskRowToTask` in `generationTaskBridge.ts` both map snake_case task rows into the same `Task` shape with nearly identical logic.
Both translators handle `params` object coercion and optional timestamp/cost/error fields independently, increasing drift risk when schema changes.
findings.suggestion: Extract a single shared task-row adapter in a boundary module and import it from both locations; add one contract test to lock mapping behavior.

findings.summary: Inline edit state abstraction has become a pass-through mega-facade with low compression and high coupling.
evidence: `useInlineEditState` aggregates many sub-hook outputs and republishes large nested structures (`canvasEnvironment`, `inpaintingState`, `transformState`, `generationState`).
`UseImageEditValueParams` mirrors a very large internal surface and `buildImageEditState` mostly reassigns fields, indicating abstraction overhead without much behavior reduction.
findings.suggestion: Split the facade into smaller domain-specific hooks (canvas, generation actions, transform/edit settings) and expose a narrower public contract consumed by `InlineEditView`.

findings.summary: Core runtime services are accessed through layered lazy proxies that obscure initialization and error provenance.
evidence: `supabase` is exported as a JS `Proxy` that forwards all operations to `getSupabaseClient()` at property access time.
`dataFreshnessManager` is a method-forwarding object wrapping lazy singleton construction for every call, then widely consumed in hooks like `useTasks.ts`.
findings.suggestion: Prefer explicit singleton initialization at app bootstrap and export direct typed instances; keep lazy init only where startup cost is measurable and document ordering guarantees.

findings.summary: Function signatures advertise weak/unknown inputs while implementation assumes concrete shapes, hiding real contracts.
evidence: `createGenerationFromTask` and `handleRegeneration` type `taskData` as `unknown` but dereference nested fields (`params`, `task_type`, `category`) without runtime guards.
Custom event payloads include broad string fields for constrained values, weakening compile-time guarantees and allowing semantically invalid payloads.
findings.suggestion: Define concrete discriminated interfaces for completion task payloads and tighten event payload unions (e.g., enumerated `side`/`priority`) so type signatures match runtime assumptions.

findings.summary: Failure handling alternates between throw, handle-and-rethrow, log-and-return-null, and silent early returns across related flows.
evidence: Client mutations and task builders often throw `Error` then call `handleError` in callback/catch paths, while some branches directly `console.error` and throw raw errors.
Server-side completion code logs via `console.error` and returns `null`/void on error branches, which differs from throw-based propagation used elsewhere.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a single cross-layer failure contract (e.g., typed error result vs throw-with-normalization), enforce with shared helpers, and remove ad-hoc `console.error` branches in favor of one reporting path.
